draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users want encouraging, upbeat language throughout the app (like a coach) rather than neutral, factual weather reporting
facade.label: Motivational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Design all copy, notifications, and feedback with celebratory language—'Crush that 5K!', 'Perfect running conditions', emoji, exclamation marks
facade.rejectImplies: Keep tone neutral and data-focused—'Temperature 62°F', 'Humidity 65%', clean sans-serif, minimal personality

facade.hypothesis: Runners expect UI animations to sync with running cadence, not arbitrary easing curves.
facade.axisTargeted: Motion timing — physiological sync vs design convention
facade.label: Pace pulses with breath
facade.acceptImplies: Layer biometric-aware animations (heart rate, stride tempo) into transitions; motion becomes feedback, not decoration.
facade.rejectImplies: Stick to standard easing (ease-in-out, spring physics); motion is visual only, divorced from body state.

evidence.hypothesis: Users want encouraging, upbeat language throughout the app (like a coach) rather than neutral, factual weather reporting
evidence.implication: Design all copy, notifications, and feedback with celebratory language—'Crush that 5K!', 'Perfect running conditions', emoji, exclamation marks

facade.hypothesis: Runners respond to energetic, colorful visual treatment that suggests motion and vitality
facade.axisTargeted: Color intensity and mood (muted vs. vibrant)
facade.label: Vibrant gradient backdrop
facade.acceptImplies: Build with bold color transitions, high saturation, dynamic visual energy
facade.rejectImplies: Explore more restrained palettes—focus on minimal color, monochrome, or earth tones instead

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer calming, natural color palettes that feel grounded rather than jarring—especially during early morning or evening runs when visual rest matters as much as performance data.
facade.axisTargeted: Warm vs Cool + Organic vs Digital
facade.label: Muted earth tones
facade.acceptImplies: The app should use subdued palette (browns, taupes, muted greens) with natural textures. Likely rejects neon alerts and bright UI.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want vibrancy, high contrast, or digital-forward aesthetics—possibly prefer bright accents, modern minimalism, or gamified visual cues that feel energizing rather than calming.

facade.hypothesis: For a running weather app, a persistent left sidebar showing current conditions + quick stats (pace zone, wind, UV) with a sticky top section keeps essential info always visible while the main area scrolls through hourly/route forecasts
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation & information hierarchy — sidebar persistence vs. tabbed switching
facade.label: Sidebar with sticky header
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent context. Explore sidebar variants: collapsible sidebar, icon-only sidebar, sidebar with cards vs. lists inside it
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers dedicated screens or tab-based switching. Pivot to full-width tabbed layout or modal/drawer patterns instead

facade.hypothesis: Runners value immediate location context alongside performance metrics, reducing cognitive load by showing both simultaneously rather than requiring tab-switching
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs key metrics — whether information is presented as a unified view or separated into focused layers
facade.label: Split screen: map + stats
facade.acceptImplies: Build a dual-pane layout where live route/weather map occupies 50% and vital stats (pace, temp, humidity) occupy the other 50%
facade.rejectImplies: Favor either a dominant full-screen map with overlay cards, or a dedicated stats dashboard that surfaces location on demand, suggesting runners prefer modal or hierarchical information separation

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance widgets on home screen vs. navigating into detailed weather views
Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity feel) vs. standard weather data
Whether user prefers smooth, flowing transitions vs. snappy, instant feedback

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance widgets on home screen vs. navigating into detailed weather views
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity feel) vs. standard weather data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, flowing transitions vs. snappy, instant feedback
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer notifications framed as friendly advice from a running companion rather than formal system alerts (e.g., 'Hey, winds picking up!' vs 'Wind speed: 12 mph')
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality — conversational/relatable vs functional/detached
facade.label: Buddy-like notifications
facade.acceptImplies: Adopt casual, second-person voice in all alerts and in-app messages; treat the app as a training partner, not a dashboard
facade.rejectImplies: Stick to neutral, data-forward language; notifications are information delivery only, not relationship-building

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind impact, and run-friendly forecasts at a glance.
